title: sideritis × vallisoletana
intro: Sideritis × vallisoletana, commonly known as Vallisoletan Rockrose, is a hybrid plant species within the genus Sideritis, belonging to the family Lamiaceae. This plant is valued for its potential health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial properties. The therapeutic actions of Sideritis × vallisoletana are attributed to its ability to act as a vasodilator, reduce stress and anxiety, and exhibit immunomodulatory effects. The bioactive constituents of this plant include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties.
benefits: Sideritis × vallisoletana has many benefits, such as its potential to aid in the reduction of inflammation and pain, making it an effective treatment for conditions like arthritis and fibromyalgia. Additionally, its antioxidant properties can help protect against cell damage, improving overall cardiovascular health and reducing the risk of certain cancers. The plant's natural antimicrobial properties can also aid in wound healing, while its anti-inflammatory effects may help alleviate symptoms of conditions like asthma and allergies. By incorporating Sideritis × vallisoletana into one's lifestyle, individuals can potentially experience improved overall health and well-being, leading to enhanced physical and mental vitality.
actions: Sideritis × vallisoletana has many therapeutic actions, such as anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, antioxidant, and antiseptic properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of conditions like arthritis, acne, and wounds. Its antioxidant properties can also protect against cell damage, improve heart health, and reduce the risk of chronic diseases like cancer and Alzheimer's. Additionally, its antiseptic properties can help prevent infections and promote wound healing, allowing individuals to recover faster and resume their daily activities. By utilizing these therapeutic actions, individuals can enjoy improved sleep, reduced stress levels, and enhanced overall well-being, enabling them to live more productive and fulfilling lives.
intro_study: According to "Daru : journal of Faculty of Pharmacy, Tehran University of Medical Sciences", Sideritis × vallisoletana benefits include antioxidant activity.
constituents: Sideritis × vallisoletana has many active constituents, such as flavonoids (luteolin, apigenin, and acacetin), phenolic acids (gallic acid and ellagic acid), terpenoids (caryophyllene oxide and borneol), and essential oils (borneol and camphor), which contribute to its medicinal properties. These constituents have been shown to exhibit anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities, helping to alleviate symptoms of anxiety and depression, improve sleep quality, and support immune function. The flavonoids and phenolic acids in Sideritis × vallisoletana have also been found to have anti-cancer properties and may help to protect against cardiovascular disease by reducing oxidative stress and inflammation. By incorporating Sideritis × vallisoletana into their daily lives, individuals may experience improved overall health and well-being, enhanced cognitive function, and a reduced risk of chronic diseases.
parts: Sideritis × vallisoletana has many medicinal parts, such as its leaves, flowers, and roots. The leaves of this plant contain flavonoids and phenolic acids, including luteolin and apigenin, which have been shown to possess anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. The flowers and roots of Sideritis × vallisoletana are known to contain terpenoids and iridoids, including sideritine and echinulin, which have been found to have antimicrobial and antispasmodic effects. The roots of this plant also contain phenolic compounds, including ferulic acid and sinapic acid, which have been reported to have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities.
preparations: Sideritis × vallisoletana has many herbal preparations, such as teas, tinctures, and infusions, which are used for medicinal purposes. Herbal tea made from Sideritis × vallisoletana is used to treat respiratory issues, including bronchitis and coughs, due to its expectorant properties. Sideritis × vallisoletana tincture is often used to treat digestive problems, such as bloating and irritable bowel syndrome, due to its anti-inflammatory and carminative properties. Additionally, an infusion of Sideritis × vallisoletana flowers has been traditionally used to help reduce anxiety and promote relaxation.
side_effects: Sideritis × vallisoletana can have side effects if used improperly, such as gastrointestinal upset, including nausea, diarrhea, and stomach pain. Prolonged or excessive use may also lead to allergic reactions, such as skin rashes, itching, and hives. Additionally, it may interact with certain medications, such as blood thinners and diabetes medications, which can result in adverse health effects. In severe cases, improper use of Sideritis × vallisoletana may lead to liver and kidney damage, requiring immediate medical attention.
